name: Information Security Management Program
description: Establish and maintain a comprehensive information security management program including policies, risk management, and governance.

subcontrols.name: An information security policy is established, approved by management, published, and communicated to all relevant parties
subcontrols.guidance: Examine security policy. Verify management approval and communication.

subcontrols.name: Information security responsibilities are clearly defined and assigned
subcontrols.guidance: Examine role descriptions and assignments. Verify accountability.

subcontrols.name: Information security policies and procedures are reviewed at planned intervals or when significant changes occur
subcontrols.guidance: Examine review records. Verify periodic reviews conducted.

subcontrols.name: Risk assessments are performed regularly to identify threats and vulnerabilities to information assets
subcontrols.guidance: Examine risk assessment reports. Verify annual cadence and scope.

subcontrols.name: A risk treatment plan is developed and implemented to address identified risks
subcontrols.guidance: Examine risk treatment plans. Verify controls implemented for identified risks.

name: Access Control
description: Control access to information assets to ensure that only authorized users can access systems and data based on business and security requirements.

subcontrols.name: An access control policy is documented and implemented based on business and security requirements
subcontrols.guidance: Examine access control policy. Verify alignment with business needs.

subcontrols.name: User registration and de-registration processes ensure authorized access assignment and timely revocation
subcontrols.guidance: Examine user provisioning/deprovisioning logs. Verify timely execution.

subcontrols.name: Privileged access rights are restricted and controlled, with elevated permissions granted on a least-privilege basis
subcontrols.guidance: Examine privileged accounts. Verify least-privilege and regular review.

subcontrols.name: Multi-factor authentication is required for remote access, privileged accounts, and access to sensitive data
subcontrols.guidance: Examine MFA configurations. Verify enforcement for required scenarios.

subcontrols.name: Access rights are reviewed at regular intervals to ensure they remain appropriate
subcontrols.guidance: Examine access review records. Verify periodic reviews conducted.

subcontrols.name: Password management requires complexity, expiration, history, and lockout controls
subcontrols.guidance: Examine password policy configurations. Verify technical enforcement.

subcontrols.name: Remote access is controlled through encrypted connections and authorized endpoints
subcontrols.guidance: Examine VPN and remote access configurations. Verify encryption and authorization.

name: Human Resources Security
description: Ensure personnel understand their responsibilities and are suitable for their roles, and that security awareness is maintained throughout employment.

subcontrols.name: Background checks are performed on all candidates for employment in accordance with relevant laws and proportional to business requirements
subcontrols.guidance: Examine screening records. Verify background checks conducted.

subcontrols.name: Terms and conditions of employment include the employee's information security responsibilities
subcontrols.guidance: Examine employment agreements. Verify security obligations included.

subcontrols.name: All employees and contractors receive appropriate security awareness education and training upon hire and regularly thereafter
subcontrols.guidance: Examine training records. Verify onboarding and annual training.

subcontrols.name: A formal disciplinary process is established for employees who have committed a security breach
subcontrols.guidance: Examine disciplinary policy. Verify security breach procedures documented.

subcontrols.name: Termination procedures include return of assets, revocation of access, and confidentiality reminders
subcontrols.guidance: Examine termination checklists. Verify access revocation and asset return.

name: Asset Management
description: Identify organizational assets and define appropriate protection responsibilities including classification, labeling, and acceptable use.

subcontrols.name: An inventory of information assets is maintained including ownership and classification
subcontrols.guidance: Examine asset inventory. Verify completeness, ownership, and classification.

subcontrols.name: Information is classified according to its value, legal requirements, sensitivity, and criticality
subcontrols.guidance: Examine classification scheme and application. Verify consistent labeling.

subcontrols.name: Acceptable use rules for information assets are documented and communicated
subcontrols.guidance: Examine acceptable use policy. Verify communication to all users.

subcontrols.name: Removable media is managed and controlled according to the classification scheme
subcontrols.guidance: Examine media management procedures. Verify controls enforced.

subcontrols.name: Media is securely disposed of when no longer needed using appropriate methods for the classification level
subcontrols.guidance: Examine disposal records. Verify destruction methods appropriate to classification.

name: Physical and Environmental Security
description: Prevent unauthorized physical access, damage, and interference to the organization's information and information processing facilities.

subcontrols.name: Physical security perimeters are defined and used to protect areas containing sensitive information and systems
subcontrols.guidance: Examine physical security controls. Verify perimeter protection.

subcontrols.name: Entry to secure areas is controlled by appropriate entry controls to ensure only authorized personnel are allowed access
subcontrols.guidance: Examine access control mechanisms. Verify authorization and logging.

subcontrols.name: Equipment is protected from environmental threats and hazards including fire, flood, temperature, and humidity
subcontrols.guidance: Examine environmental controls. Verify monitoring and protection systems.

subcontrols.name: Visitor access is controlled with sign-in/sign-out procedures and escort requirements
subcontrols.guidance: Examine visitor logs. Verify sign-in/escort procedures.

name: Communications and Operations Management
description: Ensure correct and secure operations of information processing facilities including malware protection, backup, logging, and network security.

subcontrols.name: Operating procedures are documented, maintained, and made available to all users who need them
subcontrols.guidance: Examine operations documentation. Verify currency and availability.

subcontrols.name: Anti-malware controls are implemented, regularly updated, and configured to detect and prevent known threats
subcontrols.guidance: Examine anti-malware configurations. Verify coverage and update frequency.

subcontrols.name: Backup copies of information and software are taken and tested regularly in accordance with the backup policy
subcontrols.guidance: Examine backup logs and test results. Verify regular execution and testing.

subcontrols.name: Audit logs recording user activities, exceptions, faults, and security events are produced, kept, and regularly reviewed
subcontrols.guidance: Examine log configurations and review records. Verify completeness and review.

subcontrols.name: Networks are managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications including segregation and monitoring
subcontrols.guidance: Examine network architecture and monitoring. Verify segmentation and controls.

subcontrols.name: Procedures for the management of removable media and information transfer are established
subcontrols.guidance: Examine transfer procedures. Verify secure handling documented.

name: Information Systems Acquisition, Development, and Maintenance
description: Ensure security is an integral part of information systems across the entire lifecycle including development, testing, and change management.

subcontrols.name: Security requirements are identified and documented for new information systems and enhancements to existing systems
subcontrols.guidance: Examine system requirements. Verify security requirements included.

subcontrols.name: Applications are developed using secure coding practices and reviewed for common vulnerabilities
subcontrols.guidance: Examine SDLC procedures. Verify secure coding guidelines and code review.

subcontrols.name: Technical vulnerability management includes timely identification, assessment, and remediation of vulnerabilities
subcontrols.guidance: Examine vulnerability scan reports. Verify remediation timelines met.

subcontrols.name: Changes to systems are controlled using formal change management procedures including testing and approval
subcontrols.guidance: Examine change tickets. Verify testing, approval, and rollback procedures.

subcontrols.name: Test data is carefully selected, protected, and controlled with no use of production data containing PII in test environments without appropriate safeguards
subcontrols.guidance: Examine test data management. Verify no unprotected PII in test environments.

name: Information Security Incident Management
description: Ensure a consistent and effective approach to the management of information security incidents including detection, response, and lessons learned.

subcontrols.name: Incident response procedures are established including identification, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident review
subcontrols.guidance: Examine IR plan. Verify all phases covered.

subcontrols.name: Security events are reported through appropriate management channels as quickly as possible
subcontrols.guidance: Examine reporting procedures. Verify reporting channels and timelines.

subcontrols.name: Information security incidents are responded to in accordance with documented procedures and within required notification timelines including HIPAA breach notification
subcontrols.guidance: Examine incident response records. Verify HIPAA notification timelines met.

subcontrols.name: Lessons learned from security incidents are used to improve incident response procedures and reduce likelihood of future incidents
subcontrols.guidance: Examine post-incident review records. Verify lessons learned documented and applied.

subcontrols.name: Evidence is collected, retained, and handled in a forensically sound manner for potential legal proceedings
subcontrols.guidance: Examine evidence handling procedures. Verify chain of custody maintained.

name: Business Continuity Management
description: Ensure the availability of information processing facilities and business operations during and after disruptions.

subcontrols.name: A business continuity framework is established to identify critical processes and maintain or restore operations within required timeframes
subcontrols.guidance: Examine BCM framework. Verify critical process identification.

subcontrols.name: Business impact analysis is conducted to determine priorities, recovery time objectives, and recovery point objectives
subcontrols.guidance: Examine BIA documentation. Verify RTO/RPO defined for critical systems.

subcontrols.name: Business continuity plans are developed, documented, and address all identified requirements including ePHI availability
subcontrols.guidance: Examine BC plans. Verify coverage including ePHI availability.

subcontrols.name: Business continuity plans are tested at least annually, and test results are used to update plans
subcontrols.guidance: Examine test records. Verify annual testing and plan updates.

name: Privacy Practices
description: Implement privacy controls to protect personal and health information in compliance with HIPAA Privacy Rule and applicable state regulations.

subcontrols.name: Notice of Privacy Practices is provided to individuals describing how their health information may be used and disclosed, and their rights
subcontrols.guidance: Examine Notice of Privacy Practices. Verify all HIPAA-required elements included.

subcontrols.name: Uses and disclosures of PHI are limited to the minimum necessary to accomplish the intended purpose
subcontrols.guidance: Examine minimum necessary policies. Verify implementation in systems and processes.

subcontrols.name: Individuals can access, inspect, and obtain a copy of their PHI maintained in designated record sets
subcontrols.guidance: Examine access request procedures. Verify 30-day response timeline.

subcontrols.name: Individuals can request amendments to their PHI and the covered entity responds within 60 days
subcontrols.guidance: Examine amendment request logs. Verify timely response.

subcontrols.name: An accounting of disclosures is maintained and provided upon request covering the six years prior to the request
subcontrols.guidance: Examine disclosure tracking. Verify six-year accounting capability.

subcontrols.name: Business associate agreements are in place with all entities that create, receive, maintain, or transmit PHI on behalf of the covered entity
subcontrols.guidance: Examine BAA inventory. Verify agreements in place for all business associates.

name: Compliance
description: Ensure compliance with legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements related to information security and avoid violations of any law or obligations.

subcontrols.name: All relevant statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements are identified, documented, and kept up to date for each information system
subcontrols.guidance: Examine compliance register. Verify requirements identified and current.

subcontrols.name: Information systems are regularly reviewed for compliance with security policies, standards, and technical implementation
subcontrols.guidance: Examine audit reports. Verify regular compliance reviews conducted.

subcontrols.name: Audit activities involving checks on operational systems are carefully planned and agreed to minimize disruption to business processes
subcontrols.guidance: Examine audit plans. Verify coordination and minimal disruption.

subcontrols.name: The organization maintains an ongoing authorization and continuous monitoring program for information systems
subcontrols.guidance: Examine monitoring program. Verify ongoing security assessment activities.

name: Encryption and Cryptographic Controls
description: Use cryptographic controls to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of information including ePHI.

subcontrols.name: A policy on the use of cryptographic controls is developed and implemented
subcontrols.guidance: Examine crypto policy. Verify coverage of key management and algorithm standards.

subcontrols.name: Data at rest is encrypted using NIST-approved algorithms (e.g., AES-256) including ePHI on workstations, servers, and portable devices
subcontrols.guidance: Examine encryption configurations. Verify NIST-approved algorithms.

subcontrols.name: Data in transit is protected using TLS 1.2 or higher for all network communications containing sensitive information
subcontrols.guidance: Examine TLS configurations. Verify minimum version and cipher suites.

subcontrols.name: Cryptographic key management procedures are established covering key generation, distribution, storage, rotation, and destruction
subcontrols.guidance: Examine key management procedures. Verify lifecycle controls.

name: Third-Party Management
description: Manage information security risks associated with external parties including business associates, vendors, and cloud service providers.

subcontrols.name: Risks associated with third-party access to information assets are identified and appropriate controls are implemented
subcontrols.guidance: Examine vendor risk assessments. Verify risk identification and controls.

subcontrols.name: Security requirements are established and agreed in contracts with third parties that access, process, or manage organizational information
subcontrols.guidance: Examine vendor agreements. Verify security requirements and BAAs.

subcontrols.name: Third-party service delivery is monitored and reviewed to ensure adherence to terms, conditions, and security requirements
subcontrols.guidance: Examine vendor review records. Verify ongoing monitoring and assessment.

subcontrols.name: Cloud service providers are assessed for compliance with HITRUST CSF or equivalent security framework before use
subcontrols.guidance: Examine cloud assessments. Verify HITRUST or equivalent certification.
